evidence.local_quote: This make a family more stronger and friendly.
evidence.full_sentence: This make a family more stronger and friendly.
evidence.problem_statement: Singular demonstrative/it subject followed by base lexical verb requires 3rd-person singular form.
evidence.explanation: Singular demonstrative/it subject followed by base lexical verb requires 3rd-person singular form.

evidence.local_quote: One problem of an ageing population is that government has to spent more money on health care and pensions.
evidence.full_sentence: One problem of an ageing population is that government has to spent more money on health care and pensions.
evidence.problem_statement: After have/has/had to, use base verb, not past/participle/ing.
evidence.explanation: After have/has/had to, use base verb, not past/participle/ing.

evidence.quote: a lot of knowledge and experience than
evidence.local_quote: Older peoples have a lot of knowledge and experience than young ones.
evidence.full_sentence: Older peoples have a lot of knowledge and experience than young ones.
evidence.explanation: Fixed-phrase lookup: 'a lot of knowledge and experience than' is a known IELTS error pattern. Correct: 'more knowledge and experience than'

examples.problem_statement: Comma splice — two independent clauses joined without coordinator

fine_grained_training_targets.why_this_priority: This target is selected because repeated detector rows map to Quantity and comparison expressions (COMPARATIVE_FORM, COLLOCATION).
fine_grained_training_targets.practice_focus: Practise: number of + countable nouns, amount of + uncountable nouns, fewer/less, more/greater.
practice_sequence: List all quantity expressions from the essay that were flagged.
Classify the noun as countable or uncountable.
Replace incorrect quantity word: number of (C) / amount of (U) / fewer (C) / less (U).
Practise comparative forms: more + adj/noun, adjective + -er, fewer/less.
Write 5 sentences using correct quantity expressions from the essay topic.

fine_grained_training_targets.why_this_priority: This target is selected because repeated detector rows map to Verb form and verb-pattern control (VERB_FORM, SUBJECT_VERB_AGREEMENT).
fine_grained_training_targets.practice_focus: Rewrite each damaged clause with subject + correct verb form + complement.
practice_sequence: Identify the main verb in each flagged clause.
Check: is this a base form, gerund, past participle, or infinitive context?
Rewrite the clause with the correct verb form.
For SVA errors: isolate subject, check singular/plural, match verb ending.
Practise 5 original sentences with the same verb pattern.

fine_grained_training_targets.why_this_priority: This target is selected because repeated detector rows map to Meaning recovery before discourse work (CLAUSE_STRUCTURE).
fine_grained_training_targets.practice_focus: Recover the intended proposition first, then repair grammar and vocabulary around that proposition.
practice_sequence: For each meaning-blocked sentence, write the intended meaning in simple words.
Rebuild the sentence: subject + verb + object only.
Add back modifiers one at a time, checking clarity after each.
Once meaning is stable, fix grammar and word choice.
Do not attempt argument-level feedback until meaning recovery is complete.

fine_grained_training_targets.why_this_priority: This target is selected because repeated detector rows map to Clause boundary and complex-sentence control (CLAUSE_STRUCTURE).
fine_grained_training_targets.practice_focus: Simplify broken complex sentences into accurate simple sentences, then recombine with one clear connector.
practice_sequence: Split each run-on or fused sentence into two standalone simple sentences.
Check each simple sentence for subject + verb + object completeness.
Choose ONE connector that matches the logical relation (cause, contrast, addition).
Rejoin as a controlled complex sentence.
Repeat with 3 original complex sentences using the same structure.

fine_grained_training_targets.why_this_priority: This target is selected because repeated detector rows map to Grammatical range and sentence variety (CLAUSE_STRUCTURE).
fine_grained_training_targets.practice_focus: Practise controlled use of complex sentence types: relative, conditional, concessive, and nominal clauses.
practice_sequence: Identify which complex structure types are absent or broken in the essay.
Practise each type in isolation: write 3 correct relative clauses, then 3 correct conditionals.
Combine a simple sentence with one complex clause using a target structure.
Review that the complex clause does not break the main clause meaning.
Rewrite two essay sentences to include one new controlled complex structure each.

representative_error_rows.explanation: Fixed-phrase lookup: 'a lot of knowledge and experience than' is a known IELTS error pattern. Correct: 'more knowledge and experience than'

representative_error_rows.local_quote: For example my grandmother is so older, but she is doing everything for home-things, for her it is easy and so excited things.
representative_error_rows.full_sentence: For example my grandmother is so older, but she is doing everything for home-things, for her it is easy and so excited things.
representative_error_rows.problem_statement: Do not combine more/most/so with comparative -er form.
representative_error_rows.explanation: Do not combine more/most/so with comparative -er form.

representative_error_rows.local_quote: This make a family more stronger and friendly.
representative_error_rows.full_sentence: This make a family more stronger and friendly.
representative_error_rows.problem_statement: Do not combine more/most/so with comparative -er form.
representative_error_rows.explanation: Do not combine more/most/so with comparative -er form.

representative_error_rows.local_quote: Even they can give good advice , working part-time or doing volunteer work as it possible.
representative_error_rows.full_sentence: Even they can give good advice , working part-time or doing volunteer work as it possible.
representative_error_rows.problem_statement: Modal followed directly by predicative adjective requires 'be'.
representative_error_rows.explanation: Modal followed directly by predicative adjective requires 'be'.

representative_error_rows.local_quote: And they stop working, so they need modey from the goverment, for take care with they health.
representative_error_rows.full_sentence: And they stop working, so they need modey from the goverment, for take care with they health.
representative_error_rows.problem_statement: Detect likely spelling error using resource-valid word and edit-distance candidate; resource absence is never an error.
representative_error_rows.explanation: Detect likely spelling error using resource-valid word and edit-distance candidate; resource absence is never an error.

representative_error_rows.local_quote: In many ways, if government makes good ability to older peoples, that will be the best plan to all countries.
representative_error_rows.full_sentence: In many ways, if government makes good ability to older peoples, that will be the best plan to all countries.
representative_error_rows.problem_statement: 'Good ability to' is non-standard; 'ability to' or 'capacity to' is correct
representative_error_rows.explanation: Fixed-phrase lookup: 'good ability to' is a known IELTS error pattern. Correct: 'ability to / capacity to'

representative_error_rows.local_quote: They can teach a children about culture and family traditions , which is very important nowadays.
representative_error_rows.full_sentence: They can teach a children about culture and family traditions , which is very important nowadays.
representative_error_rows.explanation: A/an is incompatible with a plural noun in this context.

representative_error_rows.quote: In many ways, if government makes good ability to older peop
representative_error_rows.local_quote: In many ways, if government makes good ability to older peoples, that will be the best plan to all countries.
representative_error_rows.full_sentence: In many ways, if government makes good ability to older peoples, that will be the best plan to all countries.
representative_error_rows.problem_statement: Comma splice — two independent clauses joined without coordinator
representative_error_rows.explanation: Two independent clauses are joined with only a comma. Use a coordinator ('and', 'but', 'so') or a semicolon, or split into two sentences.

representative_error_rows.local_quote: In conclusion, even though an ageing population can cause some problems, like a high costs and fewer workers, I believe that the advantages are also important.
representative_error_rows.full_sentence: In conclusion, even though an ageing population can cause some problems, like a high costs and fewer workers, I believe that the advantages are also important.
representative_error_rows.problem_statement: Indefinite article a/an is incompatible with plural noun head.
representative_error_rows.explanation: Indefinite article a/an is incompatible with plural noun head.

representative_error_rows.local_quote: Another issued is that the way be fewer young people working .
representative_error_rows.full_sentence: Another issued is that the way be fewer young people working .
representative_error_rows.problem_statement: Derivative ending in -ed/-ing/-ly after determiner before copula may be wrong word form.
representative_error_rows.explanation: Derivative ending in -ed/-ing/-ly after determiner before copula may be wrong word form.

fine_grained_training_targets.why_this_priority: This target is selected because repeated detector rows map to Quantity and comparison expressions (COMPARATIVE_FORM, COLLOCATION).
fine_grained_training_targets.practice_focus: Practise: number of + countable nouns, amount of + uncountable nouns, fewer/less, more/greater.
practice_sequence: List all quantity expressions from the essay that were flagged.
Classify the noun as countable or uncountable.
Replace incorrect quantity word: number of (C) / amount of (U) / fewer (C) / less (U).
Practise comparative forms: more + adj/noun, adjective + -er, fewer/less.
Write 5 sentences using correct quantity expressions from the essay topic.
example_quotes: a lot of knowledge and experience than

evidence_examples.quote: a lot of knowledge and experience than
evidence_examples.local_quote: Older peoples have a lot of knowledge and experience than young ones.
evidence_examples.full_sentence: Older peoples have a lot of knowledge and experience than young ones.
evidence_examples.problem_statement: Wrong comparative phrase
evidence_examples.explanation: Fixed-phrase lookup: 'a lot of knowledge and experience than' is a known IELTS error pattern. Correct: 'more knowledge and experience than'

evidence_examples.local_quote: For example my grandmother is so older, but she is doing everything for home-things, for her it is easy and so excited things.

fine_grained_training_targets.why_this_priority: This target is selected because repeated detector rows map to Verb form and verb-pattern control (VERB_FORM, SUBJECT_VERB_AGREEMENT).
fine_grained_training_targets.practice_focus: Rewrite each damaged clause with subject + correct verb form + complement.
practice_sequence: Identify the main verb in each flagged clause.
Check: is this a base form, gerund, past participle, or infinitive context?
Rewrite the clause with the correct verb form.
For SVA errors: isolate subject, check singular/plural, match verb ending.
Practise 5 original sentences with the same verb pattern.

evidence_examples.local_quote: This make a family more stronger and friendly.
evidence_examples.full_sentence: This make a family more stronger and friendly.
evidence_examples.problem_statement: Singular demonstrative/it subject followed by base lexical verb requires 3rd-person singular form.
evidence_examples.explanation: Singular demonstrative/it subject followed by base lexical verb requires 3rd-person singular form.

evidence_examples.local_quote: One problem of an ageing population is that government has to spent more money on health care and pensions.
evidence_examples.full_sentence: One problem of an ageing population is that government has to spent more money on health care and pensions.
evidence_examples.problem_statement: After have/has/had to, use base verb, not past/participle/ing.
evidence_examples.explanation: After have/has/had to, use base verb, not past/participle/ing.

target_validation.policy: V4 display-safety + target-family compatibility + saturation-adjusted pressure

fine_grained_training_targets.why_this_priority: This target is selected because repeated detector rows map to Meaning recovery before discourse work (CLAUSE_STRUCTURE).
fine_grained_training_targets.practice_focus: Recover the intended proposition first, then repair grammar and vocabulary around that proposition.
practice_sequence: For each meaning-blocked sentence, write the intended meaning in simple words.
Rebuild the sentence: subject + verb + object only.
Add back modifiers one at a time, checking clarity after each.
Once meaning is stable, fix grammar and word choice.
Do not attempt argument-level feedback until meaning recovery is complete.

fine_grained_training_targets.why_this_priority: This target is selected because repeated detector rows map to Clause boundary and complex-sentence control (CLAUSE_STRUCTURE).
fine_grained_training_targets.practice_focus: Simplify broken complex sentences into accurate simple sentences, then recombine with one clear connector.
practice_sequence: Split each run-on or fused sentence into two standalone simple sentences.
Check each simple sentence for subject + verb + object completeness.
Choose ONE connector that matches the logical relation (cause, contrast, addition).
Rejoin as a controlled complex sentence.
Repeat with 3 original complex sentences using the same structure.

fine_grained_training_targets.practice_focus: Practise controlled use of complex sentence types: relative, conditional, concessive, and nominal clauses.
practice_sequence: Identify which complex structure types are absent or broken in the essay.
Practise each type in isolation: write 3 correct relative clauses, then 3 correct conditionals.
Combine a simple sentence with one complex clause using a target structure.
Review that the complex clause does not break the main clause meaning.
Rewrite two essay sentences to include one new controlled complex structure each.

student_safe_rows.problem_statement: Comma splice — two independent clauses joined without coordinator

student_safe_rows.local_quote: In many ways, if government makes good ability to older peoples, that will be the best plan to all countries.
